title: calceolaria viscosa
intro: Calceolaria viscosa, also known as sticky pocketbook or sticky slipper flower, is a plant species native to South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to soothe wounds and prevent infection. The health benefits of Calceolaria viscosa include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Calceolaria viscosa can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to treat a variety of health conditions.
